items.headline: Sam Bankman-Fried officially asks Donald Trump for a presidential pardon
items.subheadline: The fallen crypto mogul serving a 25-year sentence filed a clemency petition, betting on Donald Trump's history of crypto pardons even though the president had told him not to count on one.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: U.K.'s FCA moves to allow mutual funds 10% exposure to crypto ETNs
items.subheadline: The FCA suggested allowing UK UCITS schemes and some NURS to invest in crypto ETNs in its latest quarterly consultation paper.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Crypto tax bills a work-in-progress as U.S. House lawmakers pose concerns
items.subheadline: The effort to push several tax bills is meant to be bipartisan, but the parties may not be comfortable with all details of the seven bills weighed by the panel.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.subheadline: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.
items.author: Michael Lie

items.headline: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.subheadline: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.
items.author: Tim Joslyn

items.headline: 5 corruption gaps Congress must close in the Clarity Act
items.subheadline: The most consequential crypto legislation in the world is moving forward in the U.S. Senate. As written, it leaves the United States exposed to money laundering, sanctions evasion, and conflicts of interest at the highest levels of government, argues Greytak.
items.author: Scott Greytak

items.headline: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.subheadline: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.
items.author: Samir Tabar

items.headline: Japan's three largest banks eye joint stablecoin issue by March 2027
items.subheadline: MUFG, SMBC and Mizuho will establish a council to explore operational frameworks and prepare for the issuance of stablecoins.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Zcash (ZEC), Hyperliquid (HYPE) tokens lead losses as traders bet against a bitcoin (BTC) price bounce
items.subheadline: Crypto markets are under pressure ahead of key U.S. inflation data due later Wednesday.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Crypto PACs go undefeated in June primaries as Fairshake scores bipartisan winning streak
items.subheadline: Winners included Democrats Zoe Lofgren, Ted Lieu, Dave Min and Rob Menendez, as well as Republican Sen. Mike Rounds, with Democrats accounting for 10 of the 11 victories.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: Iran war shows markets no longer sleep
items.subheadline: The latest conflict involving Iran has produced an unexpected proving ground for financial infrastructure, and an unlikely winner has emerged, argues Huang.
items.author: Annabelle Huang

items.headline: Live updates: bitcoin tumbles to $60,000 as blowout jobs data, Zcash bug keeps pressure on crypto
items.subheadline: Zcash plummetted 40% as Shielded Labs revealed a major bug that went undetected for four years
items.author: Stephen Alpher

items.headline: Live bitcoin updates: What's next for bitcoin as it faces headwinds from SpaceX, Fed rates, and Mythos
items.subheadline: Core inflation rose less than feared in May, but markets have turned lower in afternoon trade.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live Bitcoin price: BTC above $63,000 as some peg SpaceX's IPO as 'next catalyst'
items.subheadline: Stocks recovered through SpaceX's oversubscribed IPO, an Nvidia and SK Hynix chip pact, and Apple's AI reboot, while crypto trailed.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Coinbase (COIN) and Cardless unveil credit card backed by stablecoins
items.subheadline: The stablecoin-secured card is for situations where a credit card cannot be approved on an unsecured basis.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Circle (CRCL) debuts cirBTC on Ethereum to challenge Coinbase (COIN) in the wrapped bitcoin market
items.subheadline: Circle unveiled "cirBTC", a token backed 1:1 with the world's largest cryptocurrency to allow users to utilise their bitcoin wealth in DeFi protocols.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Wall Street will run entirely on the blockchain by 2030, says Brickken CEO
items.subheadline: Edwin Mata argues that EU regulations are choking local startups, leaving the U.S. and automated AI tools to run the future of tokenized finance.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: A16z, Paradigm lead $175 million investment to move global credit markets onchain
items.subheadline: Morpho secured $175 million from Paradigm, a16z crypto and Ribbit as the lending protocol seeks to become foundational infrastructure for on-chain credit markets.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Ethena (ENA) lands Janus Henderson investment in token, USDe distribution
items.subheadline: The deal underscores the trend of traditional asset managers backing DeFi infrastructure, following BlackRock's Uniswap and Apollo's Morpho investments.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Trad.Fi, W3 target $650 million in onchain private credit using AI evaluation
items.subheadline: Equipment-financing lender Trad.Fi is working with W3 to automate capital workflows and bring real-economy business lending onto public blockchain rails.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Securitize CEO says tokenized stocks could unlock a $5 trillion crypto market
items.subheadline: At a panel at ETHConf, Carlos Domingo argued that bringing stocks and exchange-traded funds onchain could unlock a market far larger than today's roughly $30 billion tokenized asset sector.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: SpaceX news: The closest thing to how SpaceX might trade is on a crypto exchange and it's down 27%
items.subheadline: The SPCX perpetual still trades above SpaceX’s $135 offer price, but it has fallen sharply from its May highs as traders mark down the first-day premium.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP market shows signs of capitulation as holders sell at loss
items.subheadline: XRP holders are capitulating, according to data tracked by Glassnode. That suggests a bottom may be near.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Security experts warn advanced AI is about to spark a hacking crisis for both crypto and banks
items.subheadline: After an AI model helped uncover a four-year-old flaw in Zcash, security researchers warn that similar bugs may be hiding across crypto and traditional financial systems.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Researcher who found Zcash's bug with AI adds Monero to his audit queue
items.subheadline: Taylor Hornby, who uncovered the Orchard flaw that sent Zcash down 38%, says other privacy coins are on his list too.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) News: Foundation cuts and departures aren't a crisis, Joe Lubin says
items.subheadline: The Consensys CEO told CoinDesk that the Ethereum Foundation's role should be narrower and more focused on stewarding the network's core technology and values.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Bitcoin spikes, then dumps, from $63,700 as analysts assess Strategy's next BTC moves
items.subheadline: BTC, ETH, XRP and others pulled back from their overnight highs as Iran-Israel tensions and oil rally triggered risk aversion in Asian stocks.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: MetaMask launches AI agent wallet with built-in security for every crypto trade
items.subheadline: The launch comes as AI agents increasingly emerge as participants in crypto markets, executing trades and managing capital on behalf of users.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Humanity Protocol token crashes more than 80% after a $32 million private-key hack
items.subheadline: The decentralized identity project said attackers compromised the keys of a foundation member and are dumping the stolen H tokens for ether.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Humanity's $36 million exploit happened because a 'multisig' lived on one laptop
items.subheadline: The compromised laptop held enough multisig keys to take over the project's bridges on two chains, a basic security failure for a startup backed by Pantera and Jump Crypto.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live BTC price updates: Bitcoin narrows early losses, returns to $62,000 as Nasdaq bounces to close down 1%
items.subheadline: Crypto prices rose on Monday following last week's crash, but the bears still appear to be in control.
items.author: Stephen Alpher

items.headline: The U.S. can’t lose the bitcoin race to China
items.subheadline: The next global power competition is not being fought over missiles alone. It’s being fought over money, and right now, China is moving aggressively to shape the future of it, argues Gooden.
items.author: Congressman Lance Gooden

items.headline: The agentic CFO in your pocket
items.subheadline: Chalom explains that retail investors have never had the opportunity to access and manage their own digital treasury desk. Until now.
items.author: Joseph Chalom

items.headline: The Clarity Act won’t lead to adoption without crypto tax reform
items.subheadline: The legislation is being framed as a turning point for U.S. crypto policy, but there’s a major piece of the puzzle being overlooked, argues Singh.
items.author: Robin Singh

items.headline: A bipartisan bridge to the future: Why the Senate must finish the job on digital Assets
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act’s recent markup proved that the momentum for regulation is there. It is imperative that Congress move the bill forward to establish rules this generation needs and a framework the next will inherit, urges Kim.
items.author: Ji Hun Kim

items.headline: Disciplined AI agents are the disruptor needed to break the exchange churn model
items.subheadline: Programmable incentives that allow independent trading agents to earn only when portfolios rise will create a fairer market for retail customers, explains Naja.
items.author: Saad Naja

items.headline: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
items.subheadline: For years, one of the most significant crypto asset markets has existed entirely outside the United States. Today, that changes, explains CFTC Chairman Selig.
items.author: Michael S. Selig

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.
items.author: Steve Bailey

items.headline: New DeFi entrant widens field of crypto political campaign funds as elections loom
items.subheadline: The Defend Developers PAC is aiming to contribute to lawmakers who seek to shield crypto developers from legal vulnerabilities.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: JPMorgan sees shrinking window for U.S. crypto market structure overhaul
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act still faces several legislative hurdles, and disputes over stablecoin yield have emerged as a key sticking point, the bank said.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Russia sanctions British teenager for alleging A7A5 use in funding Ukraine war
items.subheadline: The A7A5 stablecoin was designed to bypass sanctions imposed on Russia following its invasion of Ukraine in 2022.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: OCC chief says Democrats applying sole political pressure in World Liberty charter choice
items.subheadline: The regulator rejected claims he's doing President Trump's bidding during a congressional hearing that also addressed the GENIUS Act stablecoin push.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto Clarity Act in spotlight for bad-actor provisions as Senate process grinds forward
items.subheadline: The industry has been trying to make a case this week the Clarity Act provides law enforcement strong tools to combat illicit finance involving cryptocurrency.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: U.S. House tax committee weighs crypto bills, including relief for small transactions
items.subheadline: Seven draft bills are being circulated by the House Ways and Means Committee ahead of a hearing next week, including proposals to ease small-gain, mining and staking burdens.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: A quick review of the Ways and Means tax bills: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: The House Ways and Means Committee is gearing up for its big tax push.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: The U.S. CPI scenario that could send the BTC price tumbling below $60,000: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: Your day-ahead look for June 10, 2026
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Botanix bet big on 'Bitcoin DeFi.' Its shutdown suggests users never cared
items.subheadline: The project cited market conditions and a broader indifference among the cryptocurrency industry to establishing greater utility on the Bitcoin network.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: U.S. inflation data better than hoped, boosting BTC
items.subheadline: Headline inflation rose an expected 0.5% in May, but the beat on the core rate — which cuts out food and energy costs — is pleasing markets.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Solana (SOL) news: World Series of Poker adds SOL payments for tournament buy-ins
items.subheadline: The move marks an integration of blockchain-based payments into a major live sporting and gaming event.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: CoinDesk 20 index drops 1.4% as all constituents decline
items.subheadline: Bitcoin Cash (BCH) fell 4.1% and NEAR Protocol (NEAR) shed 4.3%, leading the index lower.
items.author: CoinDesk Indices

items.headline: Strategy (MSTR) news: Michael Saylor gets into public back-and-forth with critics
items.subheadline: Strategy's BTC Yield fell from 13.0% to 12.8% following its latest bitcoin purchase, prompting a debate on X over whether the purchase was dilutive to shareholders.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Prediction markets get first U.S. rule proposal as CFTC pursues contract reviews
items.subheadline: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission opened a proposed rule for public comment that sets an approach to determine contracts in the "public interest."
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Kalshi now requires users to reveal employers as it fights insider trading and market manipulation
items.subheadline: The prediction markets platform introduced new measures on Tuesday that it said will apply to markets it considers are likely to face higher risks of insider trading and abuse.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Mastercard prepares agentic commerce platform for a future where AI agents make payments
items.subheadline: The company is working with Coinbase, Stripe and other companies to build trusted payment systems for AI-driven commerce.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Netomi CEO says $5 trillion AI customer experience market could boost stablecoin demand
items.subheadline: Puneet Mehta, a former high-level Wall Street engineer and data scientist, said the rapid expansion of AI enterprise software will increase demand for stablecoins and blockchain.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Coinbase-backed Stand With Crypto calls on members to campaign against banks blocking digital asset transactions
items.subheadline: Stand With Crypto UK is telling its members to file formal complaints with high-street banks over sector-wide transfer bans.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: BlackRock and Fidelity are quietly turning bitcoin ETFs into a two-firm market
items.subheadline: BlackRock's IBIT and Fidelity's FBTC are attracting the vast majority of new bitcoin ETF money, leaving smaller funds increasingly sidelined as institutional investors consolidate around the industry's largest players.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) developers are exploring new token standards as privacy returns to focus
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at where privacy is headed in the Ethereum ecosystem
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: BTC, ETH, SOL price news: Bitcoin has reached a deep bear-market valuation zone
items.subheadline: Two widely watched gauges show capitulation, but the analyst flagging them warns the slow grind comes next.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP price news: Ripple-linked token above $1.10 as ETF inflows rise
items.subheadline: XRP bounced from recent lows on a late volume surge, though the token continued to lag the broader crypto market as futures traders positioned around key support levels.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin DAT buying collapses from $500 million per day to nearly negligeble
items.subheadline: Bitcoin is losing institutional demand from two directions: Spot ETFs and digital asset treasuries.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: BTC updates: Soft core inflation gave crypto a bounce, but only bitcoin held up on the week
items.subheadline: May CPI ran hot on energy and cooler underneath, lifting majors on Thursday, though ether and the large alts are still down 6% to 8% over seven days.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
